facts.context: Which of the following describes the cluster of blood capillaries found in each nephron in the kidney?
You notice that your patient's submandibular lymph nodes are enlarged. You would look for potential infection sites in the
facts.truth: hard palate, upper lip, upper central incisor and lower first molar.
distractors: hard palate, upper lip and upper central incisor.

facts.context: A patient suffers damage to the orbit in a road traffic incident resulting in damage to the third cranial nerve. Which of the following signs will be present?
facts.truth: Pupillary dilatation and a lateral strabismus
distractors: Pupillary constriction and a medial strabismus
Pupillary dilatation and a medial strabismus
Pupillary constriction and a lateral strabismus

facts.context: Which of the following statements is true of the temporomandibular joint?
facts.truth: Proprioceptive information is carried by the masseteric and auriculotemporal nerves.
distractors: It has an articular disc of hyaline cartilage
Its articular surfaces are covered by hyaline cartilage
Proprioceptive information is carried by the chorda tympani and deep temporal nerves

facts.context: Which muscles are normally active during quiet inspiration?
distractors: External and internal intercostal muscles and diaphragm

distractors: superficial temporal artery crosses the maxilla.
superficial temporal artery crosses the orbital rim.

facts.context: Food is prevented from entering the larynx during the pharyngeal phase of swallowing by
Which of the following conditions is characterized by incompetence of the esophageal sphincter?
Which of the following is the large bone found superior to the patella and inferior to the ischium?
The mucosa of the upper larynx is innervated by the
facts.truth: internal laryngeal nerve which is the afferent limb of the cough reflex.
distractors: external laryngeal nerve which is the afferent limb of the gag reflex.
internal laryngeal nerve which is the afferent limb of the gag reflex.
external laryngeal nerve which is the afferent limb of the cough reflex.

facts.context: The bones of the viscerocranium develop initially by
facts.truth: intramembranous ossification and follow the somatic growth pattern.
distractors: endochondral ossification and follow the somatic growth pattern.
endochondral ossification and follow the neural growth pattern.
intramembranous ossification and follow the neural growth pattern.

facts.context: Which of the following structures accompany the oesophagus through the diaphragm?
A possible effect of damage to the third cranial nerve by trauma to the orbit is
In the brain stem, pathways for:
facts.truth: skilled motor movements decussate in the pyramids.
distractors: nociception decussate in the medial lemniscus
skilled movements decussate in the medial lemniscus
discriminative touch decussate in the pyramids.

facts.context: Which of the following are mucus-producing glands located on each side of the vaginal opening?
When an emergency opening into the airway is required, the cricothyroid membrane should be pierced immediately
Which orbital wall is most likely to collapse in a 'blow out' fracture?
The walls of the maxillary sinus are sinus is related to
facts.truth: the floor of the orbit, the upper posterior teeth and the infratemporal fossa.
distractors: the floor of the orbit and the upper posterior teeth.
the floor of the orbit, the upper posterior teeth, the infratemporal fossa and the hard palate.

facts.context: Macrostomia results from failure of fusion of
Osteoclasts
facts.truth: resorb bone and differentiate from monocytes.
distractors: deposit bone and differentiate from periosteal mesenchymal cells.
resorb bone and differentiate from periosteal mesenchymal cells.
deposit bone and differentiate from monocytes.

facts.context: The head of the sixth rib articulates with
facts.truth: The inferior articular demifacet of T5 and the superior articular demifacet of T6.
distractors: The inferior articular facet of T5 and superior articular facet of T6.
The inferior articular demifacet of T5 and superior articular facet of T6.
The superior and inferior demifacets of T6.

facts.context: A successful infraorbital nerve block will produce anaesthesia of the
facts.truth: maxillary anterior teeth, their labial gingivae and the upper lip.
distractors: maxillary anterior teeth and their labial gingivae.
maxillary anterior teeth, their labial gingivae, the upper lip and anterior hard palate.

facts.context: Which of the following foramina pierce the sphenoid bone?
facts.truth: The foramen ovale, rotundum and spinosum
distractors: The foramen ovale, rotundum and spinosum and foramen lacerum
